tasks.exit_criteria: Migration applied on test DB; emissions.event_audit exists append-only; new alerts columns present; backfill plan for historical rows (auto_closed=false, acknowledged_at=email_received as proxy) documented.
tasks.deliverable: Numbered migration file (e.g. migrations/NNN_emissions_kpi_instrumentation.sql) + updated schema doc.

tasks.title: Measure classification accuracy vs. the labeled set
tasks.description: Run the live classifier (rule cascade + Maxwell LLM fallback per FR-11/FR-12) over the held-out validation set and produce the accuracy report: overall accuracy, per-class precision/recall/F1 (Process Emissions / Unexpected / Undetected), full confusion matrix, and the abstention/escalation behavior (confidence <0.65 -> Undetected -> MRO review — verify it abstains rather than guesses). Report accuracy both raw and adjusted for the inter-annotator ceiling from DATA-8. Compare against the PRD >=92% Phase-1 target. Feed any systematic error modes back to the Agent workstream as tuning items WITHOUT touching the held-out set.
tasks.entry_criteria: DATA-8 labels complete; the live classifier reachable against historical evidence (read paths or bootstrap S3 drops available).
tasks.exit_criteria: Accuracy report delivered: overall + per-class precision/recall, confusion matrix, abstention behavior, vs. the 92% target and the human ceiling; error modes handed to the Agent workstream.
tasks.deliverable: Classification accuracy report (confusion matrix + per-class metrics + ceiling-adjusted accuracy).

tasks.title: Lock the 60-day pilot success criteria & measurement plan
tasks.description: Convert PRD §11's six criteria into a signed, measurable acceptance contract: (1) >=95% events ingested without human intervention; (2) median MTTA <=15 min from kg/hr threshold (subject to DATA-9's clock decision); (3) >=40% auto-closed as Process Emissions; (4) Sierra's monthly report generated entirely from the agent store (no Outlook transcription); (5) no API incident requiring Darko's team to roll back access; (6) Devin and Sierra confirm in writing they would not return to manual. For each, define the exact metric source (which query/endpoint), the measurement window (60 days production), and the threshold. Bind each to the DATA-5 scorecard tiles. Get Clovis + Darko + Sierra + Devin sign-off.
tasks.owner_person_or_role: Steve Ridder (Taikun) drafts; Clovis, Darko, Sierra, Devin (TEEP) sign
tasks.entry_criteria: Revised baselines (DATA-3), scorecard live (DATA-5), and MTTA-clock decision (DATA-9) available.
tasks.exit_criteria: Signed success-criteria & measurement-plan doc: each of the 6 criteria mapped to a metric source, window, and threshold, and bound to a scorecard tile; signed by Clovis, Darko, Sierra, Devin.
tasks.deliverable: Signed 60-day pilot success-criteria & measurement plan.

tasks.title: Build the KPI metrics layer & pilot scorecard endpoint
tasks.description: Implement the agent-performance KPIs that PRD FR-35 lists but that are not computed today: MTTA (acknowledged_at - emission_start, split by P1/P2 thresholds), MTTR / dispatch-to-close (field_closed_at - dispatched_at), auto-close rate (auto_closed=true / total), classification-mix, per-system API error rate (from event_audit status_code), and TaskHub round-trip latency (open->close). Extend the existing KpiSummary model + GET /api/emissions/summary rather than inventing a parallel ontology, and add a dedicated pilot-scorecard endpoint that renders the PRD §11 success criteria as live pass/fail tiles. Surface in the existing emissions.html reporting tab (thin frontend, no client-side logic). NO hardcoded numbers — all from queries over alerts + event_audit.
tasks.entry_criteria: DATA-4 migration applied; agent beginning to write acknowledged_at / auto_closed / dispatch timestamps (or backfill proxy in place).
tasks.exit_criteria: GET /api/emissions/summary returns MTTA, MTTR, auto-close rate, per-system API error rate; pilot-scorecard endpoint renders the 6 PRD §11 criteria as live tiles; values reconcile to hand-computed checks on a sample week; visible in emissions.html.
tasks.deliverable: Extended KpiSummary + metrics queries + pilot-scorecard API endpoint + reporting-tab tiles.

tasks.title: Define & build the methane-avoided estimator (instrumented, not a static deck number)
tasks.description: Turn the deck's methane-avoided figure (~70 t CH4 over the 22-day sample; ~33,000 t CO2e/yr at GWP 28 IPCC AR5) into a reproducible, query-driven estimate. Method per the deck: per-event vented mass = median(or per-event) emission_rate_kgh x leak_duration, where leak_duration = MTTA + resolution time; avoided = baseline_duration_vented - maxwell_duration_vented; aggregate across events and annualize from the run-rate. Parameterize GWP (28, AR5) and the duration model as config, not constants. Document every assumption (rate is sensor-driven and unchanged; only duration shrinks) so the number survives HSE/EPA scrutiny. Validate the estimator reproduces the deck's ~70 t on the Jan sample, then re-run on full history.
tasks.owner_person_or_role: Taikun engineering with Brent (TEEP HSE field lead) reviewing the emissions method
tasks.entry_criteria: DATA-3 baselines and DATA-5 MTTA/MTTR metrics available; GWP/AR5 convention confirmed with Brent.
tasks.exit_criteria: Estimator reproduces the deck's ~70 t CH4 / ~33,000 t CO2e/yr on the Jan sample within rounding; produces an updated full-history figure; all assumptions documented and reviewed by Brent.
tasks.deliverable: Methane-avoided estimator (config-driven) + assumptions memo + reproduced & full-history figures.

tasks.title: Define the labeled validation set & freeze it
tasks.description: Design a stratified, frozen validation set carved from the historical pull for measuring classification accuracy. Preserve the real class imbalance but over-sample the rare classes so they are measurable: Undetected was only 4/168 in Jan, and field-cleared (~43.5%) and the long-tail equipment/epa_identifier vocab values are sparse. Target ~300-400 labeled events spanning multiple months/seasons. Define the label schema (ground-truth resolution_type + equipment + equipment_component + epa_identifier + cleared_location + a binary 'should-have-auto-closed') and store it in a new emissions.alert_labels table keyed by emission_id. Critically: this set is HELD OUT of all prompt/rule tuning to prevent leakage. Document sampling method and rationale.
tasks.owner_person_or_role: Taikun engineering (sampling design) with Devin (TEEP) on labeling scope
tasks.entry_criteria: DATA-2 history ingested; agreement with Devin on adjudication availability and on the label vocabulary (Sierra's controlled vocabs).
tasks.exit_criteria: emissions.alert_labels table created; ~300-400 stratified events selected with documented sampling method; set frozen (snapshot hash recorded) and marked held-out from tuning.
tasks.deliverable: Validation-set definition doc + emissions.alert_labels schema + frozen sample manifest (with hash).

tasks.title: Human ground-truth labeling pass (Devin + Sierra adjudication)
tasks.description: Run the actual labeling. Devin (MRO triage) and Sierra (HSE reporting) adjudicate the held-out validation set: confirm or correct resolution_type / equipment / equipment_component / epa_identifier / cleared_location and mark whether each event should have been auto-closed from the office. Provide a lightweight labeling view (reuse the Event Detail screen) so they label against the same evidence the agent sees. Capture inter-annotator agreement on a 30-event overlap to quantify label noise (this caps the achievable accuracy — if humans only agree 94%, a 92% target is reasonable; if they agree 99%, push higher). Resolve disagreements by discussion, log final labels to emissions.alert_labels.
tasks.owner_person_or_role: Devin + Sierra (TEEP), facilitated by Taikun engineering
tasks.entry_criteria: DATA-7 frozen set ready; labeling view available; Devin & Sierra time blocked.
tasks.exit_criteria: All ~300-400 events labeled in emissions.alert_labels; inter-annotator agreement computed on the 30-event overlap and recorded; disagreements adjudicated and logged.
tasks.deliverable: Completed ground-truth labels + inter-annotator-agreement memo (sets the realistic accuracy ceiling).

tasks.title: Reconstruct the true MTTA baseline (kg/hr threshold, not email proxy)
tasks.description: Resolve the most important measurement gap: the published 5.2h baseline is the email-delay proxy (email_received - emission_start), but the PRD KPI G1/§11 target is measured 'from kg/hr threshold crossing'. Sierra's xlsx has no threshold-crossing timestamp. Work with the Sensirion-API workstream to obtain, from Sensirion device data, the actual time the kg/hr threshold (FR-3, default 1 kg/hr) was crossed for the historical events, and recompute the true baseline MTTA. If Sensirion cannot supply historical threshold timestamps, document that the baseline remains an email-delay proxy and that the post-Maxwell MTTA (webhook-driven) is measured on a different clock — a comparability caveat that must be stated in the success criteria.
tasks.entry_criteria: Sensirion-API workstream (SEN-*) has established whether device-level threshold timestamps are available for historical events; DATA-2 history loaded.
tasks.exit_criteria: Either: true threshold-based baseline MTTA computed and stored, OR a documented decision that MTTA stays an email-delay proxy with the comparability caveat written into the success-criteria doc.
tasks.deliverable: MTTA baseline reconciliation note (true-threshold vs. email-proxy) feeding DATA-3 and DATA-11.

tasks.title: Operate the live pilot scorecard during the 60-day window
tasks.description: Once Maxwell is live on TEEP APIs (post-cutover), run the scorecard continuously over the 60-day production window. Weekly KPI review with Clovis/Devin/Sierra; track MTTA, MTTR, auto-close rate, ingestion completeness, per-system API error rate, and a rolling shadow-accuracy check (sample of live closures spot-checked against MRO/LO ground truth) to detect drift from the historical accuracy. Maintain the free-text -> category mapping table reviewed by Sierra weekly (per the PRD risk row). Flag any criterion trending to miss early enough to act.
tasks.entry_criteria: Maxwell live on TEEP APIs (Cutover complete); success criteria signed (DATA-11); scorecard deployed to the live environment.
tasks.exit_criteria: 60 days of continuous scorecard data captured; weekly reviews held and logged; shadow-accuracy spot-checks recorded; mapping table maintained; any at-risk criterion escalated with lead time.
tasks.deliverable: Weekly KPI review logs + a continuously-updated live pilot scorecard + shadow-accuracy tracker.

tasks.title: Run the Phase-1 -> Phase-2 go/no-go review
tasks.description: At the end of the 60-day window, produce the go/no-go decision package measured against the DATA-11 signed criteria: each of the 6 criteria pass/fail with evidence, the measured-vs-target table for every PRD §7 KPI, the realized methane-avoided figure (DATA-6 estimator on live data), the final classification accuracy on live traffic, and a recommendation on Phase-2 targets (auto-close 40%->65%, accuracy 92%->95%, MTTA <5min). Capture the written confirmations from Devin and Sierra (criterion 6). Present to Clovis + Darko for the formal decision.
tasks.owner_person_or_role: Steve Ridder (Taikun) authors; Clovis + Darko (TEEP) decide
tasks.entry_criteria: DATA-12 60-day window complete; live accuracy (DATA-10 method re-run on live closures) and methane-avoided (DATA-6) available.
tasks.exit_criteria: Go/no-go package delivered and decided by Clovis + Darko; each criterion pass/fail with evidence; Devin & Sierra written confirmations captured; Phase-2 target recommendation accepted or revised.
tasks.deliverable: Phase-1->Phase-2 go/no-go decision package + recorded decision.

workstreams.name: Integration Testing, Cutover, Go-Live and Operate

tasks.exit_criteria: Sequencing plan + RACI signed off by Darko and Taikun; go-live order, base-URL flip mechanism, and email-fallback trigger documented.

tasks.title: Weekly mapping-table review with Sierra (operate window)
tasks.description: Per PRD risk mitigation (HSE/EPA category drift) and FR-29, run a weekly review with Sierra of the free-text-to-category mapping table (LO note -> resolution_type / equipment / equipment_component / epa_identifier) during the operate window. Capture new LO phrasings, adjust the mapping config (not code — columns are configuration), and re-validate that the monthly Excel export still matches her exact 22-column template. This keeps classification accuracy on track toward the >=92% Phase-1 target and ensures her report stays a drop-in replacement.
tasks.entry_criteria: Production live; at least one week of real triage decisions recorded.
tasks.exit_criteria: Weekly review held for the full operate window; mapping-table changes logged and applied as config; monthly export confirmed matching Sierra's template each cycle.
tasks.deliverable: Weekly mapping-review log + versioned mapping-table config history

tasks.title: Operate-window monitoring + incident handling against success criteria
tasks.description: Run the 60-day operate window watching the success criteria live: >=95% Sensirion events ingested without human intervention, median MTTA <=15 min, >=40% auto-closed, Sierra's report generated entirely from the agent store, and zero API incidents forcing Darko to roll back access. Triage any per-system degradation via the notification channel + degraded-mode behavior; log every API incident and its resolution. Capture the operating data that, with the historical pull, firms the KPI commitments (the 22-day sample was too short).
tasks.entry_criteria: Production live; monitoring + notifications verified (CUTOVER-11/12).
tasks.exit_criteria: 60 days of traffic processed; KPI trend reports produced; incident log shows no roll-back-forcing API incident (or documents any that occurred + fix); accumulated data ready for KPI recalibration.
tasks.deliverable: 60-day operate report (KPI trends + incident log) measured against the 6 success criteria

tasks.title: Pilot review + Phase-2 go/no-go decision
tasks.description: At the end of the 60-day window, run the pilot review with Clovis, Steve, Darko, Sierra, and Devin against the 6 success criteria. Secure the written confirmation from Devin and Sierra that they would not return to the manual process (criterion 6). Present KPI actuals vs targets, recommend whether to recalibrate the auto-close target from the Phase-1 40% toward the Phase-2 65% using the accumulated operate-window + historical data, and produce the Phase-2 proposal (operator response handler, LLM free-text reconciliation when rule confidence <0.7, SMS/Teams escalation channels, pre-event Cygnet anomaly detection).
tasks.entry_criteria: 60-day operate report complete (CUTOVER-18); weekly mapping reviews complete (CUTOVER-17).
tasks.exit_criteria: Pilot review held; all 6 success criteria assessed with evidence; Devin + Sierra written sign-off captured; Phase-2 go/no-go recorded with a scoped Phase-2 proposal.
tasks.deliverable: Pilot review readout + Phase-2 go/no-go decision + Phase-2 scope proposal

critical_path.why: Locks the four foundational decisions (gateway tech, auth method, bootstrap transport, Path A/B). Until these are made nothing on either side can build; it is the literal first gate of the whole program.

IT-security sign-off on cross-account S3 data sharing determines Path A vs Path B for the asset registry and whether the bootstrap track (which unblocks all Taikun build) is even permitted. Sahir-owned, enterprise lead time.

The signed S3 bootstrap bucket is the physical channel for every per-system drop; no bucket means no bootstrap data means Taikun is blocked on the gateway, collapsing the parallelism the whole plan depends on.
The frozen drop spec (shapes byte-identical to teep-api.yaml) is what makes cutover config-only. If shapes drift the config-only-cutover invariant breaks and every adapter needs rework at cutover.

The ProCount well catalog is the canonical asset spine every other system (Cygnet/Sensirion/Carte/FMP) binds onto. A late spine cascades into all binding and every cross-system enrichment fan-out; gated by the still-TBD IFS owner.
Building the spine (with API#-based Strategy A binding) is the prerequisite for binding all other systems; the canonical asset_id resolution gates the agent's enrichment fan-out for every alert.

Sensirion device→pad/well binding (reference-only) is the keystone: Sensirion is the 168/168 alert origin, so a wrong or missing binding breaks every downstream fan-out. Go-live gate for the triage path.

Composing the durable emissions_triage_close_loop workflow is where all stage tools become a working end-to-end agent; nothing can be cutover or rehearsed until this exists and runs through the engine + gateway.
The 4-scenario simulator run on the test VM is the proof the agent is correct and idempotent before any live traffic; it is the verification gate that makes the per-system cutovers low-risk.

Sebastian's net-new TaskHub write endpoints + 24h Idempotency-Key store are the highest-effort, highest-risk net-new piece and the only write target; the field-dispatch close-the-loop (~27% of events) cannot go live without it. Email-fallback de-risks but this is the true tail.
The TaskHub write idempotency/duplicate-dispatch soak is the one cutover where a bug puts a duplicate real field job in front of a lease operator; it gates enabling write in production.

SSO production cutover (gated by Sahir/TEEP IT Entra app-reg + admin-consent latency) is a hard prerequisite for full governed production access — no shared/manual credentials may reach prod. Enterprise-process long-pole.

Bedrock production-LLM cutover gates the auto-close/classification path for governed production (every classification is an LLM call); gated by TEEP AWS model-access approval. Direct-Anthropic fallback de-risks but production posture requires it.
The full end-to-end production rehearsal of all 4 scenarios against the real stack is the single GO/no-go before declaring production; it is the convergence point of every other critical-path task.
Production go-live starts the 60-day operate clock; the entire success-criteria measurement window (and thus the end date and Phase-2 decision) cannot begin until this lands.

milestones.name: Kickoff decisions locked & long-lead approvals fired
milestones.gate_criteria: GW-1 four foundational decisions signed; Entra app-reg (SSO-2) and Bedrock model-access (BEDROCK-2) requests submitted with named owners; IT-security data-sharing/Path-A decision in writing (GW-2); IFS Merrick/ProCount owner named (IFS-1); Sensirion/FMP/catalog contracts frozen and lint-clean; historical-data request TEEP-signed (DATA-1).

milestones.name: Bootstrap live — first real drops flowing & adapters building
milestones.gate_criteria: Signed S3 bucket reachable by Taikun with first contract-valid drops for all 5 systems + per-system catalogs (GW-3/4/5); Taikun S3 poller + read adapters green against bootstrap (GW-6); drop shapes validate against teep-api.yaml with zero drift defects open.

milestones.name: Canonical asset spine + cross-system binding validated
milestones.gate_criteria: ProCount spine built and Cygnet/Sensirion/Carte/FMP bound onto it (REG-8/9/10/11); end-to-end 168-alert fan-out coverage meets per-system mention rates with zero number collisions and ≥80% auto-link on fuzzy systems (REG-12); review queue worked with SME sign-off (REG-13).

milestones.name: Agent feature-complete & simulator-green on mock/bootstrap
milestones.gate_criteria: emissions_triage_close_loop durable workflow loads in the engine + ReactFlow editor and drives all three branches end-to-end (AGENT-14); 4-scenario simulator (auto-close/dispatch/monitor/timeout) passes with idempotency + per-system failure isolation (AGENT-16); Sierra export validated against her real file (REPORT-12); classification accuracy measured vs the held-out labeled set (DATA-10).

milestones.name: Read systems live via config-only gateway cutover
milestones.gate_criteria: Sensirion live webhook + poll-fallback verified (CUTOVER-5/SEN-12); Cygnet-via-intermediary signal fidelity validated (CUTOVER-6); ProCount/Carte read validated (CUTOVER-7); all read systems flipped to the gateway base URL via config with verified S3 rollback (CUTOVER-8); office-cleared auto-close path runnable on real data.

milestones.name: Governance + write gates passed (SSO, Bedrock, TaskHub-write)
milestones.gate_criteria: SSO corporate login role-mapped per persona on test then prod (SSO-9/10, CUTOVER-13); Bedrock decision-parity ≥92% + within latency + governance sign-off + production cutover (BEDROCK-6/8/10, CUTOVER-14); TaskHub write idempotency soak shows zero duplicates across 50+ replayed writes or email-fallback armed (CUTOVER-9, FMP-14); monitoring + alerting demonstrated (CUTOVER-11/12).

milestones.name: Production GO & 60-day operate clock starts
milestones.gate_criteria: Full 4-scenario production rehearsal green against the real stack with Sierra's 22 columns auto-populating (CUTOVER-15); signed GO decision; live events processing end-to-end; 60-day clock started with recorded start date and on-call ownership assigned (CUTOVER-16).

milestones.name: Phase-1 → Phase-2 go/no-go
milestones.gate_criteria: 60 days of production traffic processed; all six PRD §11 success criteria assessed with evidence (≥95% auto-ingested, median MTTA ≤15min, ≥40% auto-closed, Sierra's report from the agent store, no roll-back-forcing API incident, Devin+Sierra written sign-off); realized methane-avoided + live accuracy reported; Phase-2 scope proposal accepted (CUTOVER-19, DATA-13).

consolidated_risks.risk: Microsoft Entra enterprise app-registration + admin consent is owned/gated by central TotalEnergies group IT (not the Barnett BU) and takes 1-3+ weeks, blocking the SSO production gate and pushing full governed go-live past the deck window.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: File the app-reg request in week 0-1 as a blocking task; resolve the consent-owner decision in SSO-1 and escalate via Darko immediately if central IT owns it. Keep the Taikun-managed login + read-system soak running so the pilot clock can still start; SSO gates only full production cutover, not read-system verification.

consolidated_risks.risk: AWS Bedrock Claude model-access approval in the TEEP account lags (hours-to-days plus possible TotalEnergies procurement/security review), blocking the production-LLM cutover.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: Submit the model-access request first at kickoff (BEDROCK-2); run the entire pilot on the existing direct-Anthropic gateway route as the fallback so go-live is never blocked on Bedrock — only the governed-inference cutover slips, not the pilot.

consolidated_risks.risk: TaskHub is the only net-new API with no external API today; Sebastian's read+write+webhook build (~26 dev-days) is the critical-path tail and likely overruns the deck's 8 weeks.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: Decouple sides: Taikun develops fully against Prism mock + S3 bootstrap so it is cutover-ready early; the config-toggled email-to-MRO fallback keeps field events flowing if write slips; sequence read before write and write last in the go-live order.

consolidated_risks.risk: A duplicate POST to TaskHub on a transient retry creates a duplicate real field-dispatch task in front of a lease operator — the one bug class with physical-world, trust-eroding consequences.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: Contract-mandatory 24h Idempotency-Key replay store (key+body-hash, not key-only) built jointly by Sebastian+Darko; Taikun replays a stable key per logical write; gate write go-live behind a dedicated zero-duplicate soak across 50+ replayed writes before any real LO receives tasks.

consolidated_risks.risk: The chosen Cygnet intermediary (esp. FMP ~30-min poll) is too coarse to reproduce intra-event pressure transients the 4h@5m series needs, or omits casing/compressor tags, degrading classification accuracy and auto-close below the ≥92% / 40% Phase-1 targets.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: Run the 95-alert resolution-impact replay (SCADA-9) before committing; default to a historian/curated-export feed where one exists; mark missing tags nullable (no faked values); route ambiguous coarse-cadence cases to Undetected/MRO rather than mis-auto-closing; hold the historian fallback (SCADA-10) ready and renegotiate the SCADA-attributable KPI slice honestly if needed.

consolidated_risks.risk: Nubo→TEEP path is poll-based (no native Nubo webhook), so TEEP re-emits on its own cadence (FMP reportedly ~30-min), capping end-to-end latency and gutting the MTTA <10min KPI even though Taikun's webhook is instant.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: Resolve webhook-vs-poll-re-emit in SEN-1; if no native webhook, push TEEP to poll Nubo at the true device cadence (not the 30-min FMP cadence) and re-emit on threshold-cross immediately; measure real MTTA at cutover and restate the KPI honestly if the path caps it.

consolidated_risks.risk: ProCount/Carte (IFS Merrick) has no named TEEP owner — discovered by mining notes, owned by nobody on the call — and ProCount is the canonical asset spine everything binds onto, so a late owner stalls the spine, the registry, and every cross-system fan-out.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: Make owner assignment the first kickoff action (IFS-1); escalate to Clovis as ops lead; sequence the ProCount spine dump first and build registry ingest against R2Q data in parallel so Taikun is not idle; engage IFS Merrick support interim.

consolidated_risks.risk: TEEP has no API gateway today and standing one up from zero plus IT-security data-sharing sign-off is the single biggest non-Taikun schedule risk; the bootstrap channel depends on it.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: Front-load the gateway tech decision (GW-1) and IT-sec sign-off (GW-2) in week 0-1; run the bootstrap S3 track fully in parallel so Taikun is unblocked regardless of gateway timeline; communicate a realistic 3-5 week gateway build with cutover trailing.

consolidated_risks.risk: Bootstrap S3 JSON shapes drift from teep-api.yaml (extra/missing fields, casing), breaking the config-only-cutover promise and forcing adapter rework at cutover across multiple systems.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: Freeze shapes with example files + schema_version in every manifest (GW-4); Taikun validates each drop against the Prism/Schemathesis contract in CI before consuming, failing loudly on drift (GW-6, CUTOVER-3); same adapter validated against both mock and bootstrap.

consolidated_risks.risk: All headline KPIs (MTTA 5.2h→<10min, 0→40% auto-close, ≥92% accuracy, ~70t CH4 avoided) are extrapolated from a 22-day, 168-alert sample with the Undetected class at only 4/168, so targets may be set against a non-representative seasonal distribution and the minority-class accuracy claim is statistically fragile.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: Secure the 6-12 month historical pull (DATA-1); recompute baselines + seasonality (DATA-3); over-sample rare classes in the frozen held-out validation set (DATA-7); report accuracy relative to the inter-annotator ceiling (DATA-8); treat the 60-day operate window as the firming data and recalibrate before committing.

consolidated_risks.risk: The published 5.2h MTTA baseline is an email-delay proxy, not a kg/hr-threshold-crossing measurement; if Sensirion cannot supply historical threshold timestamps, the before/after comparison is on different clocks, undermining the headline KPI.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: DATA-9 attempts true threshold reconstruction via the Sensirion-API workstream; failing that, state the proxy caveat explicitly in the signed success criteria and report post-Maxwell MTTA as threshold-to-classified with a documented clock change.

consolidated_risks.risk: Fully-autonomous auto-close (writing final regulatory fields + sending Sierra's closeout email with no human touch) closes events incorrectly, eroding trust or mis-stating an HSE/EPA record.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: Ship behind a 3-state auto-close gate defaulting to shadow; require confidence ≥0.85; graduate shadow→human-confirm→autonomous only after Devin reviews divergences and Clovis signs off; Sierra can override any auto-populated field with an audited change.

consolidated_risks.risk: The 24h durable monitor/timeout fails to survive an agent restart/redeploy, leaving dispatched events stuck open with no escalation.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: Run the monitor/timeout on the durable substrate (DBOS) — explicitly NOT exempt from durable execution; resume in-flight events from status='enriching'; test forced-restart-mid-monitor in AGENT-16; rely on supervisord auto-restart on the VM.

consolidated_risks.risk: emissions.event_audit and the agent-performance KPI columns do not exist in the current schema; if the agent does not consistently write acknowledged_at/auto_closed/dispatch timestamps, the live scorecard reports nulls and the signed success criteria become unmeasurable.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: Land the instrumentation migration early (DATA-4/AGENT-2/3) and agree the field-write contract jointly between DATA and AGENT; backfill proxies (acknowledged_at=email_received, auto_closed=false) for historical rows so the scorecard never silently shows zero.

consolidated_risks.risk: Disabling interim auth at SSO cutover locks everyone out if the EntraID config is subtly wrong (tenant GUID typo, mismatched byte-for-byte redirect URI behind CloudFront, expired secret), and resetting the VM off origin/main has previously broken auth.
consolidated_risks.mitigation: Cut over only after acceptance passes on the test VM with identical config; retain a documented out-of-band break-glass internal admin; provide instant rollback (re-enable internal login + restart); set AUTH_PUBLIC_BASE_URL for the CloudFront origin split; never reset the VM off origin/main.

consolidated_decisions.question: Which gateway technology, primary auth method (OAuth2 client-credentials vs mTLS), bootstrap transport (S3 drops vs SFTP vs read-replica), and asset-registry path (A vs B) will TEEP use? (Four linked foundational decisions.)
consolidated_decisions.recommended_default: AWS API Gateway in TEEP's account; OAuth2 client-credentials with 1h tokens + emissions.read/write scopes; signed S3 JSON drops (Option A); asset-registry Path A (Taikun ingests catalogs). Fall back to Path B only if IT-security blocks data sharing.
consolidated_decisions.needed_by: Kickoff week 0 (GW-1/GW-2) — gates the entire gateway + bootstrap build

consolidated_decisions.question: Is TaskHub write (POST/PATCH) confirmed in Phase-1 scope, does Maxwell auto-close or does the LO close via UI with a webhook, and can TaskHub emit an outbound webhook or must Taikun poll?
consolidated_decisions.recommended_default: Write IS in Phase-1 scope (required for the ~27% dispatch close-the-loop); LO closes via the TaskHub UI with the task.updated webhook recording it (lower trust barrier); build the HMAC webhook if feasible but build the 5-min poll backstop regardless; email-to-MRO fallback if write isn't live by week 4.
consolidated_decisions.needed_by: Kickoff (FMP-1) — gates the entire write build

consolidated_decisions.question: Does Nubo offer a native webhook to TEEP (true event-speed) or must TEEP poll Nubo and re-emit (latency capped by TEEP's poll cadence)? And what is the authoritative device poll cadence (device_poll_seconds) and PPM/kg-hr sample resolution?
consolidated_decisions.recommended_default: If no native webhook, TEEP polls Nubo at the confirmed true device cadence (not the 30-min FMP cadence) and re-emits on threshold-cross immediately; proceed on a 60s interim cadence default for series resolution and backfill once Nubo confirms.
consolidated_decisions.needed_by: Kickoff (SEN-1/SEN-2) — before contract freeze and KPI sign-off

consolidated_decisions.question: Which existing intermediary sources the 6 Cygnet/SCADA signals (FMP ~30-min poll / ProCount documented Cygnet integration / historian or curated S3 export), and does it carry casing pressure + compressor suction/discharge at usable cadence?
consolidated_decisions.recommended_default: Use a CygNet historian/curated-export feed if one exists (closest to 4h@5m fidelity, cleanest contract fit); fall back to FMP's ~30-min cache for state corroboration only and accept reduced series resolution as a documented Phase-1 limitation, conditional on the SCADA-9 replay clearing targets.
consolidated_decisions.needed_by: Kickoff week 0 (SCADA-2/3) — gates the adapter build and KPI grounding

consolidated_decisions.question: Who is the accountable TEEP business + technical owner for ProCount/Carte (IFS Merrick), does ProCount expose the 14-digit API number per well (deterministic Strategy A binding), and should Carte be dropped and served from ProCount OData?
consolidated_decisions.owner: Clovis to assign; IFS Merrick support (TEEP)
consolidated_decisions.recommended_default: Assign the production-accounting/RRC-Form-PR lead with Michelle confirming and IFS Merrick support as technical contact; confirm API# presence (Strategy A if present, fuzzy Strategy C + normalization if not); drop the separate Carte API and serve injection_rate from ProCount OData, keeping the /v1/carte/ contract slot.
consolidated_decisions.needed_by: Kickoff week 0-1 (IFS-1/IFS-3, REG-3) — gates the canonical asset spine

consolidated_decisions.question: What is the TEEP/TotalEnergies Entra tenant GUID, who owns Enterprise App registration + admin consent (Barnett BU vs central group IT), and what is the production Taikun hostname + exact byte-for-byte redirect URI?
consolidated_decisions.recommended_default: Use the platform's existing OIDC EntraID provider (SAML out of scope); obtain the tenant GUID from Sahir; assume Sahir drives within the BU but escalate via Darko if central IT owns consent; default to the demo.taikunai.com host callback unless TEEP prefers a TEEP-branded host, setting AUTH_PUBLIC_BASE_URL for the CloudFront split.
consolidated_decisions.needed_by: Kickoff week 0-1 (SSO-1/SSO-2) — enterprise consent lead time is the top schedule risk

consolidated_decisions.question: What is the TEEP AWS account id, the Bedrock region, which Claude model(s) are enabled, and is cross-account AssumeRole-from-Taikun-us-east-1 acceptable for Phase 1 (vs gateway in TEEP's VPC)?
consolidated_decisions.recommended_default: Confirm account/region (do not fabricate); prefer Bedrock region us-east-1 to match Taikun's gateway and minimize latency; enable a Claude 3.5 Sonnet-class primary + Claude 3 Haiku-class fast tier; use cross-account AssumeRole + external-id for the pilot and defer co-located gateway to Phase 2 unless Darko mandates it now.
consolidated_decisions.needed_by: Kickoff/Bootstrap (BEDROCK-1/2/3) — model-access approval is a long-pole

consolidated_decisions.question: How are dispatch + API-failure notifications delivered (email / Teams / PagerDuty) and to which named TEEP contact, and what kg/hr trigger threshold X qualifies an event for dispatch (FR-3)?
consolidated_decisions.recommended_default: Email to a named Darko/MRO distro as the Phase-1 default (add Teams/PagerDuty if TEEP supplies an integration key); default trigger threshold 1 kg/hr until TEEP HSE confirms.
consolidated_decisions.needed_by: Build (GW-17/CUTOVER-12/FMP-10) — before observability + email fallback ship

consolidated_decisions.question: Who provides Sierra's exact live HSE/EPA column list, sheet scope (alerts-only vs 5-sheet workbook), controlled vocabularies, and rulings on the dirty values ('Process Emissions ' trailing-space dupes, blank Resolution Type)?
consolidated_decisions.recommended_default: Use the 'Sensirion Alert Data' 22-column set, alerts-sheet-only for Phase-1, with canonicalized vocabularies (trim/collapse dupes, default blank Resolution Type to 'Undetected'); keep 'Process Emissions' as the legitimate catch-all equipment value; treat all of this as config so a mid-pilot change is a config edit.
consolidated_decisions.needed_by: Kickoff (REPORT-1/AGENT-1) — gates the export + the agent write-map

consolidated_decisions.question: When will the 6-12 month historical pull be delivered, must the kg/hr-threshold MTTA baseline be reconstructed vs the email proxy, and does the 60-day pilot clock start at API-availability cutover or at end-to-end-live steady state?
consolidated_decisions.recommended_default: Target 12 months (min 6), Sensirion + notes first; reconstruct true threshold MTTA via Sensirion device data if available, else publish the proxy with a documented clock caveat; start the 60-day clock only after the read-system auto-close path is live end-to-end on real data.
consolidated_decisions.needed_by: Kickoff/Bootstrap (DATA-1/9, before DATA-11/12) — gates KPI sign-off

consolidated_decisions.question: What is the go-live posture for autonomous auto-close (shadow only / human-confirm / autonomous for Process Emissions), and is the platform's 3-role model extended for the HSE-reporting persona?
consolidated_decisions.recommended_default: Default to shadow at go-live, graduate to human-confirm after Devin reviews ~1-2 weeks of divergences, then autonomous for Process Emissions only with Clovis sign-off (dispatch/escalate can go autonomous earlier, lower blast radius); realise HSE-reporting as viewer + reporting/export entitlement rather than a schema change.
